sections.heading: Dashboard Overview
sections.content: The Dashboard (tab_dashboard.py) is the primary command center for NEXUS. It provides a live stereo video feed, real-time telemetry from the VisionEngine, hardware status, engine controls, and live-tuning sliders — all without needing to restart the engine. It is built as a QWidget that subscribes to two signals from the VisionEngine QThread: frame_ready(np.ndarray) for video and telemetry_ready(dict) for metrics.

sections.heading: Layout Structure
sections.content: The Dashboard is split into two panels using a QHBoxLayout:
list: Left Panel (stretch=3): The TacticalVideoWidget — displays the combined stereo frame with bounding boxes, skeleton overlays, and tracking markers in real time.
Right Sidebar (fixed 360px): A scrollable QScrollArea containing the NEXUS CORE control panel — status, metrics, sliders, and buttons.

sections.heading: Telemetry Metrics Panel
sections.content: The metrics grid shows 7 live indicators, each updated on every telemetry_ready signal emission:

sections.heading: System Info Labels
sections.content: Four read-only labels show the current engine configuration. They are updated each time you switch to the Dashboard tab via showEvent():

sections.heading: Target Status Indicator
sections.content: A large status badge changes dynamically based on the tracking state received in telemetry:
rows: Engine running but no target locked yet
Engine is stopped — default state on launch

sections.heading: Hardware Status (HW) Indicator
sections.content: A compact label below the metrics shows the real-time ESP32 connection status. It is updated every telemetry cycle by polling engine.hardware.is_connected():
rows: Connection lost — auto-reconnect may be retrying
User clicked RECONNECT, attempting to link

sections.heading: Aiming Control Sliders
sections.content: Three sliders allow real-time tuning of the targeting pipeline. Changes take effect immediately on the running engine without a restart. Values are saved to settings.json on each change.
list: OFFSET (0–100 px): Number of pixels below the shoulder midpoint keypoints to place the final aim dot. 0 = aim at shoulder center, 50 = aim at chest center, 100 = aim at stomach level. Stored as CONFIG['AIM_OFFSET'].

SMOOTHNESS (0–100%): Controls Exponential Moving Average (EMA) output smoothing on servo commands. 0 = raw snap to every detection, 100 = ultra-smooth but slow to follow fast movement. Best value for reducing servo jitter: 40–60. Stored as CONFIG['AIM_SMOOTHNESS'].

FILTER (0–100%): Controls Kalman filter measurement trust. 0 = trust raw AI bounding box coordinates entirely, 100 = heavy filtering (useful in noisy/crowded environments). Stored as CONFIG['AIM_FILTER'].

sections.heading: Trim Controls
sections.content: Pan Trim and Tilt Trim sliders apply a global degree offset to every servo command. Range: −18.0° to +18.0° in 0.1° steps (slider stores value × 10 as integer for precision). Use after laser calibration to fix small systematic misalignment. Values are synced from CONFIG whenever they change (including changes made from the Hardware tab or auto-refine).
sections.code:
# Applied in hardware_link.py — TacticalSerial.send_target()
final_pan  = pan  + CONFIG['PAN_TRIM']   # e.g., 90.0 + (-2.5) = 87.5°
final_tilt = tilt + CONFIG['TILT_TRIM']  # e.g., 150.0 + 1.0  = 151.0°

# How Dashboard reads back the slider value:
pan_trim_value  = self.dash_pan_trim.value()  / 10.0  # int 25 → 2.5°
tilt_trim_value = self.dash_tilt_trim.value() / 10.0

sections.heading: Engine Start / Stop
sections.content: The ▶ START ENGINE button launches the VisionEngine thread. A QProgressBar appears while the engine is initializing (loading model, opening cameras, connecting hardware). It hides automatically on the first telemetry_ready signal, confirming the engine is running.
rows: Engine initializing — button disabled, bar pulses
Calls engine.stop() — safe shutdown of all threads

sections.heading: Laser Toggle
sections.content: The LASER button sends a toggle command to the hardware link via engine.toggle_laser(). Critically, the user intent is preserved across ESP32 disconnections and reconnections — if the ESP32 drops and comes back, the laser state is automatically restored to match the button.
rows: Dark card background with cyan border

sections.heading: Alarm Toggle
sections.content: The ALARM button sends a buzzer enable/disable command: engine.toggle_alarm() which transmits B1 or B0 to the ESP32 firmware.
rows: Dark card background with orange border

sections.heading: RECONNECT Button
sections.content: Calls engine.reconnect_hardware() which bypasses the 5-second auto-reconnect cooldown and forces an immediate connection attempt. Useful when you plug in the ESP32 after starting the engine.
sections.alertContent: The RECONNECT button only works while the engine is running. If the engine is stopped, reconnect hardware from the Hardware Control tab instead.

sections.heading: Telemetry Signal — Full Dictionary
sections.content: The complete telemetry dict emitted by VisionEngine on every frame:
sections.code:
# Emitted every frame by: self.telemetry_ready.emit(data)
data = {
    'fps':              float,   # Current inference FPS
    'latency':          float,   # ms per frame
    'pan':              float,   # Current commanded pan angle (°)
    'tilt':             float,   # Current commanded tilt angle (°)
    'conf':             float,   # AI detection confidence (0.0–1.0)
    'depth':            float,   # Stereo-estimated distance (m), 0.0 if uncalibrated
    'status':           str,     # 'LOCKED' or 'SCANNING'
    'calibration_mode': str,     # e.g. '✅ FULLY CALIBRATED' / '⚠️ UNCALIBRATED RAW'
}
